rows.code_license: BSD-3-Clause (skimage SLIC) + GPL-3.0 (PyMaxflow) — BK maxflow algorithm is research/non-commercial
rows.notes: LANDMINE. SLIC superpixels (skimage, BSD-3) are fine, but the graph-cut step via PyMaxflow pulls a GPL-3.0 dependency AND the wrapped Boykov-Kolmogorov maxflow is licensed research/non-commercial only (paid UCLB license for commercial use). Double blocker for a commercial-safe Apache-2.0 package. AL

rows.code_license: BSD-3-Clause (skimage.segmentation.random_walker; scipy BSD-3, optional pyamg MIT)
rows.notes: Random-walker segmentation. All deps permissive (BSD/MIT). Optional pyamg multigrid solver is MIT. No weights, no GPL. Commercial-safe.

Live-Wire / Intelligent Scissors. Shipped in OpenCV core (not contrib/nonfree) under Apache-2.0. The original Mortensen-Barrett livewire patent (US 5,995,115, filed 1997) is expired, and OpenCV distributes the implementation freely. No weights, no GPL. Commercial-safe.

Pure algorithm, no checkpoint. cv2.grabCut uses OpenCV's OWN internal min-cut/max-flow (gcgraph.hpp), NOT PyMaxflow, so no GPL is pulled in. GrabCut had a historical Microsoft patent but OpenCV ships it under Apache-2.0 and it is widely used commercially. Commercial-safe.
Marker-controlled watershed. Either backend (OpenCV or skimage) is permissive. No weights, no GPL. Commercial-safe.

rows.code_license: BSD-3-Clause (scikit-learn RandomForest; optional skimage features BSD-3)
rows.notes: Interactive per-pixel RandomForest classifier trained on user scribbles at runtime (model fit on the fly, no pretrained checkpoint shipped). All deps permissive BSD-3. Commercial-safe.

SLIC superpixels + RandomForest with smoothing. Unlike slic-graphcut, this variant uses sklearn (BSD-3) not PyMaxflow, so NO GPL. If a graph-cut/CRF smoothing step is later added, ensure it uses a permissive maxflow (scipy/networkx), not PyMaxflow/gco. As described: commercial-safe.

Classic edge operators (Canny/Sobel/Scharr/Laplacian) are permissive in both backends; no weights. CAVEAT: if 'edge-plus' instead uses OpenCV-contrib ximgproc StructuredEdgeDetection (Dollar's Structured Forests), that model file historically carried a non-commercial research license and would need

SAM 1 (Meta, Apr 2023). Code+weights both Apache-2.0; openly downloadable, not gated. Trained on SA-1B which is a research-only dataset, but Meta released the trained checkpoints under Apache-2.0 -> commercial use permitted. ViT-B backbone.
SAM 1 ViT-L. Same licensing as sam-vit-b: Apache-2.0 code+weights, ungated, commercial OK. SA-1B training data is research-only but the released weights are Apache-2.0.
SAM 1 ViT-H (largest, sam_vit_h_4b8939.pth). Apache-2.0 code+weights, ungated, commercial OK. Research-only SA-1B dataset does not restrict the Apache-2.0 weights.

SAM 2 (Meta, Jul 2024). Checkpoints, demo code and training code are Apache-2.0. NOT a separate 'SAM 2 License' and not Llama-style; only the eval code is BSD-3. SA-V video training data is research/CC BY 4.0 but weights are Apache-2.0 -> commercial OK. Ungated. Hiera-T backbone.
SAM 2 Hiera-S. Apache-2.0 code+weights, ungated, commercial OK. Eval code BSD-3 only; SA-V dataset research-only does not encumber the Apache-2.0 weights.
SAM 2 Hiera-B+ (base-plus). Apache-2.0 code+weights, ungated, commercial OK. Same SAM 2 licensing as the other sizes.
SAM 2 Hiera-L (largest). HF model card explicitly apache-2.0. Code+weights Apache-2.0, ungated, commercial OK. Eval code BSD-3 only.

SAM 2.1 (improved Meta checkpoints, late 2024) served from the same facebookresearch/sam2 repo. Checkpoints + training code Apache-2.0, ungated, commercial OK. No separate license for 2.1 weights vs 2.0. Hiera-T.
SAM 2.1 Hiera-S. Apache-2.0 code+weights, ungated, commercial OK. Same SAM 2.1 licensing as the other sizes.
SAM 2.1 Hiera-B+ (base-plus). Apache-2.0 code+weights, ungated, commercial OK.

SAM 2.1 Hiera-L (largest, best quality). HF card explicitly apache-2.0. Code+weights Apache-2.0, ungated, commercial OK. Also offered commercially via AWS SageMaker JumpStart, confirming commercial intent. Eval code BSD-3 only.

MobileSAM = TinyViT image encoder distilled from SAM + original SAM mask decoder. Both code and released checkpoint are Apache-2.0, openly downloadable from GitHub releases (not gated). Encoder distilled on a ~1% SA-1B subset; SA-1B itself is a research-license dataset, but Meta/authors released the

Meta (Yunyang Xiong et al.). Repo LICENSE confirmed Apache-2.0; vit-tiny / vit-small checkpoints released Apache-2.0, not gated. SAMI pretraining leveraged ImageNet (MAE) + SA-1B (research dataset), but Meta released weights under Apache-2.0. Commercial-safe.

Distilled checkpoints: HF 'xinghaochen/tinysam' is tagged apache-2.0 and is NOT access-gated; the distilled checkpoints carry NO additional restriction beyond Apache-2.0. Post-training-quantized (INT8) variant of TinySAM. HF 'xinghaochen/Q-TinySAM' card states apache-2.0 and is NOT gated; same Apache-2.0 repo. The quantized checkpoints carry no extra restriction beyond Apache-2.0. Commercial-safe.

PRIMARY LANDMINE. EdgeSAM (S-Lab, NTU; chongzhou96) is licensed under NTU S-Lab License 1.0, which is NON-COMMERCIAL / research-only: it permits redistribution and use ONLY for non-commercial purposes and requires contacting the authors for any commercial use. rows.weights_license: Unspecified for checkpoints; mixed backbones (HRNet permissive; SegFormer/MiT non-commercial)

rows.weights_license: Effectively non-commercial: checkpoints initialized from Facebook MAE ViT weights (CC BY-NC 4.0)
rows.notes: Decisive landmine: MAE pretrained ViT backbones (facebookresearch/mae) are CC BY-NC 4.0 (non-commercial) — confirmed. SimpleClick checkpoints are derivatives of those NC weights, so the published weights are non-commercial regardless of the MIT code, and also carry COCO+LVIS dataset terms. Per rule

Verified Apache-2.0 + ungated via HF API. Trained/fine-tuned on COCO (standard COCO image-license caveat applies but weights released Apache-2.0). Smaller seg tier — fully commercial-safe and redistributable.
Verified Apache-2.0 + ungated via HF API. COCO-pretrained. Commercial use, mirroring and fine-tuning all permitted.
Verified Apache-2.0 + ungated. COCO-pretrained. Commercial-safe and redistributable.

Verified Apache-2.0 + ungated via HF API. IMPORTANT distinction: for DETECTION, sizes Nano..Large are Apache but XL/2XL (rfdetr[plus], DINOv3) are PML-1.0; the SEGMENTATION 'large' checkpoint is plain Apache-2.0. Commercial-safe, redistributable.

Open-vocab detector. Code + weights both Apache-2.0 and ungated. Swin-B (cogcoor) was pretrained on a research dataset mix (COCO/Objects365/GoldG/Cap4M/OpenImages/RefCOCO); IDEA nonetheless released the weights under Apache-2.0 and the model is broadly used commercially. Mirroring/bundling permitted
Swin-T 'OGC' checkpoint (groundingdino_swint_ogc). Apache-2.0 code + weights, ungated. Pretrained on Objects365/GoldG/Cap4M; released Apache-2.0 by IDEA. Commercial use and redistribution OK.
Alias of the HF transformers Swin-T checkpoint (same as grounding-dino-swin-t). Apache-2.0, ungated, freely redistributable. Commercial-safe.

Original (non-HF-transformers) IDEA checkpoint groundingdino_swinb_cogcoor.pth. Same Apache-2.0 weights release and same research-dataset training mix as grounding-dino-swin-b. Ungated; bundling permitted.
Original IDEA checkpoint groundingdino_swint_ogc.pth (Swin-T). Apache-2.0 code + weights, ungated. Commercial use and redistribution OK.

Fast Segment Anything (CASIA-IVA-Lab), built on Ultralytics YOLOv8 backbone -> AGPL-3.0 contaminates both code and weights. No commercial license offered. Copyleft poison-pill: bundling weights would force AGPL on whole package, so do not mirror. Project already flags DO_NOT_ADD. Keep external basel
Larger FastSAM variant, same AGPL-3.0 code+weights as fastsam-s. AGPL copyleft, no commercial offering. Excluded from permissive/commercial-safe core; external baseline only.

All Ultralytics YOLO trained models are AGPL-3.0 by default. Commercial closed-source use requires a paid Ultralytics Enterprise License (hence 'conditional', NOT free). Under AGPL alone, integrating into a commercial package forces full source disclosure. Cannot mirror/bundle into permissive core;
YOLO11 segmentation weights AGPL-3.0; commercial use only via Ultralytics Enterprise License. Same copyleft constraint as YOLOv8-seg. Exclude from commercial-safe core; external baseline only.

Newest Ultralytics generation (2025/2026), confirmed AGPL-3.0 on HF Ultralytics/YOLO26. Enterprise License required for closed-source commercial use. Treat identically to YOLOv8/YOLO11 seg: external baseline only, no bundling.

Open-vocabulary detector. Original Tencent repo is GPL-3.0 (README informally says 'supported for commercial usage' but GPL copyleft obligations still apply); Ultralytics path is AGPL-3.0. Either way strong copyleft -> commercial use only via separate licensing/source disclosure ('conditional'). Pre

Classic CODE-vs-WEIGHTS split: super-gradients library is Apache-2.0, but the YOLO-NAS pretrained checkpoints (AutoNAC-generated) carry Deci's restrictive non-commercial license. So even though code is permissive, the published weights forbid commercial use -> commercial_use_allowed=no and cannot be
